steps.title: Identify the key criticism
steps.content: Lady Howard criticizes Villars through the phrase “too rigidly sequestered”—excessive sheltering creates false, romantic views of the world. The phrase “it is time that [Evelina] should see something of the world” indicates he has kept her overprotected.

steps.title: Match the criticism to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C identifies Villars as “overly protective” and states Evelina would benefit from “a greater variety of experiences”—this captures the problem directly: protection prevents the real-world exposure necessary for realistic understanding.

The other three either narrow the scope (A's focus on “other young people”), introduce unsupported claims (B attributes mistrust of the Mirvans to Villars), or misidentify the source of idealism (D attributes it to Villars' influence rather than sequestration itself).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
